channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Claude just got a skill upgrade and it’s coming for your workflows
item.description: Model builders are moving beyond simple AI chatbots to creating comprehensive assistants that, in the words of AI dignitary Ethan Mollick, “do real work” in enterprise workflows.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Threat actors are spreading malicious extensions via VS marketplaces
item.description: Careless developers publishing Visual Studio extensions to two open marketplaces have been including access tokens and other secrets that can be exploited by threat actors, a security vendor has found.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Sex could become the next big business opportunity for AI companies
item.description: ChatGPT will be able to have kinkier conversations after OpenAI CEO Sam Altman announced the artificial intelligence company will soon allow its chatbot to engage in “erotica for verified adults.” OpenAI won’t be the first to try to profit from sexualized AI.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Hi-fi legend Quad's new super-powerful, super-connected stereo amp could be all the amp you need for the rest of your life
item.description: Money No Object: Quad launches its "finest-ever" integrated amp, and it's future-proofed in a ton of ways
item.dc:creator: Carrie Marshall, TechRadar

item.title: Toonstar’s new ‘Uncle Roger’ cartoon embraces AI—but slop it’s not
item.description: “The gyoza needs to look a little whiter. It’s too pink.” Nigel Ng is genially micromanaging the look and feel of Fried, an animated series that will premiere on YouTube later this year.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Shaping enterprise talent strategies in the age of AI
item.description: AI, and in particular agentic AI, present an opportunity to rethink the nature of work, reshaping teams and blurring the line between technology and talent leadership.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: From heart health to health equity, Dr. David Goff is shaping science and inspiring public service
item.description: "We've had a lot of success, but we're now having backsliding, and there's a lot of work to be done," said Dr. David Goff. The post From heart health to health equity, Dr. David Goff is shaping science and inspiring public service first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: Manufacturing, research and industrial hub may replace San Jose offices
item.description: A "high-end industrial campus" is being eyed at a north San Jose site, a project that is expected to create new jobs.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: Apple’s Big Bang AI moment is approaching
item.description: Want to use artificial intelligence (AI) that maintains privacy and doesn’t need a lake full of water or industrial electricity generator to make an emojii? Apple has that.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The Sonoma County connection to an off-the-grid Nobel Prize winner
item.description: Fred Ramsdell, who won a Nobel Prize in medicine, made headlines by missing the announcement because he was camping.
item.dc:creator: Charles Swanson, The Mercury News

item.title: How to make workers happier with less pay
item.description: Workers want remote work so badly they’re willing to take a 25% pay cut to get it, according to a new study from economists at Harvard University, Brown University, and UCLA. The study published this week was conducted with Levels.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI pushes CIOs to redefine IT roles for the future
item.description: AI is already reshaping the IT job landscape, with layoffs and demand for AI skills creating a mismatched market for IT skills. But the technology is also contributing to possible contraction for IT workforces as enterprises IT leaders find themselves redefining IT roles in the wake of AI’s rise.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Big Tech is paying millions to train teachers on AI, in a push to bring chatbots into classrooms
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — On a scorching hot Saturday in San Antonio, dozens of teachers traded a day off for a glimpse of the future. The topic of the day’s workshop: enhancing instruction with artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Getac launches world's fastest rugged laptop - and, for the first time in decades, we have an AMD-powered rugged notebook
item.description: Getac’s S510AD introduces AMD’s AI power to rugged laptops, blending durability, sustainability, and field-ready performance for professional users.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: Chamber of Commerce sues to block $100K H-1B visa fees
item.description: The U.S. Chamber of Commerce sued the Trump administration Thursday over its decision to raise the fee for H-1B work visa applications to $100,000, arguing that President Trump exceeded his authority in altering the visa program.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Nvidia, Infineon partner for AI data center power overhaul
item.description: Infineon’s Power & Sensor Systems division is teaming up with Nvidia to upgrade the outdated power architecture of AI data centers and replace them with a centralized high-voltage DC power setup.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta details cutting-edge networking technologies for AI infrastructure
item.description: Meta shared details about its AI and networking advances at this week’s 2025 OCP Global Summit in San Jose, Calif.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Major companies, including Google and Dior, hit by massive Salesforce data breach
item.description: Google, Dior, Allianz and dozens of other companies lost sensitive customer data in Salesforce-related breaches affecting millions of records across multiple sectors.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Arm joins Open Compute Project to build next-generation AI data center silicon
item.description: Chip designer Arm Holdings plc has announced it is joining the Open Compute Project to help address rising energy demands from AI-oriented data centers. Arm said it plans to support companies in developing the next phase of purpose-built silicon and packaging for converged infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Energy Department offers $1.6 billion loan guarantee to upgrade transmission lines across Midwest
item.description: The Department of Energy says it has finalized a $1.6 billion loan guarantee to a subsidiary of one of the nation’s largest power companies to upgrade nearly 5,000 miles of transmission lines across five states, mostly in the Midwest, for largely fossil fuel-run energy.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Daly, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: As Broadcom becomes its latest major ally, this graph shows how OpenAI made itself too big to fail by securing hundreds of billions from the world's largest tech companies
item.description: Broadcom joins Nvidia, AMD, and Microsoft in OpenAI’s trillion-dollar AI infrastructure collaboration revealed in social media image.
item.dc:creator: Wayne Williams, TechRadar

item.title: Apple adds game-changing iPhone document feature that users waited years to see
item.description: Apple launches iOS 26 with new Preview app that combines document editing, PDF annotation and scanning features into one streamlined iPhone experience.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Booker courts red state voters online, urging them to join ‘No Kings’ protests
item.description: Sen. Cory Booker (D-N.J.) is pressing red state voters to join local “No Kings” protests Saturday and put pressure on their GOP legislators to end the government shutdown through his outreach with social media influencers and creators in Florida and North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft: Russia, China increasingly using AI to escalate cyberattacks on the US
item.description: Russia, China, Iran and North Korea are using artificial intelligence to improve their cyberattacks on U.S. companies, governments and individuals, according to new research from Microsoft.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Death of kidnapped South Korean student spurs talks with Cambodia to tackle online scams
item.description: PHNOM PENH, Cambodia (AP) — Cambodian and South Korean officials agreed Thursday to cooperate in combating online scams, after the death of a South Korean student who was reportedly forced to work in a scam center in Cambodia triggered public outrage in South Korea.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and SOPHENG CHEANG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top Army official using ChatGPT to make military decisions: Report
item.description: An expert warned that, no matter how fast the tech can give an answer, nothing can replace human judgment when it comes to military calls.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Using Valkey on Azure and in .NET Aspire
item.description: Big changes to the license used by the popular open source key/value store Redis prompted a fork, with the launch of Valkey. In the time since that fork in March 2024, the two projects have diverged.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How to run an R data visualization chatbot you can talk to
item.description: Typing questions into a chatbot is nice, but speaking often feels more natural. In fact, some experts encourage people to talk to generative AI instead of typing, in part to get out of the habit of using them as glorified search engines.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Flying drones take aim at retail theft
item.description: Flying drones could help retailers fight a 93% increase in theft rates as Flock Safety promotes airborne security systems to track suspects and deter crime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
